body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
irrlicht_observes: n/a

## What the scenario requires

The `permission_hooks` primitive here is specifically a *classifier*: the agent inspects each tool call and selectively prompts the user only for the risky ones, while safe calls run without ever entering `waiting`. The user-observable distinguisher is two divergent paths from the SAME session: safe → `ready → working → ready` (no `waiting`), risky → `working → waiting → working → ready` after approval. The scenario is the safe-path branch; the risky-path branch is owned by `tool-gate-permission-prompt`. Both branches must be driven by a content-aware classifier, not a blanket policy (that is the explicit "Not to be confused with" carve-out vs `auto-executed-tool-call`, which auto-approves everything, and `tool-gate-permission-prompt`, which gates everything).

## Why agent_supports is partial, not no or yes

Aider HAS a permission gate, but it is uniform, not classifier-based. All confirmations funnel through a single `IO.confirm_ask` (aider/io.py:807). Its entire decision logic is the run flag: `if self.yes is True: res = "y"` / `elif self.yes is False: res = "n"` / else interactively prompt (io.py:866-868). `--yes-always` (aider/args.py:760, help: "Always say yes to every confirmation") sets `self.yes = True` blanket. There is no branch that inspects the tool/command content to decide safe-vs-risky — a `strings`-equivalent grep of the installed 0.86.2 package for `classif|risky|dangerous|safe.?command|allowlist|denylist` matched only an unrelated docs file (ollama.md), and the only auto-* flags are `--yes-always` and `--auto-accept-architect` (neither content-aware).

So the verdict is not `no` (aider genuinely has a permission-confirmation mechanism and can run the safe topology) and not `yes` (the mechanism is blanket, not the selective classifier the scenario's primitive demands). `partial` with the gap named is the honest call, ratifying the prior matrix note "/yes-always is blanket, not classifier-based."

## Why irrlicht_observes is n/a

The aider adapter is built around `--yes-always`. `Source` is `FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}` (agent.go:45-51); the markdown parser's `toolCall()` emits the tool open and close in a single `tool_result` event "so the state classifier sees a completed tool call without a permission window" (parser.go:231-242). No parser path emits a held-open tool_use / permission / `waiting` event — the only states aider can produce are `working` (turn open) and `ready` (turn_done via IdleFlush or error). `capabilities.json` carries `"permission_hooks": false`.

The safe-path topology (`ready → working → ready`, no `waiting`) would in fact be observable — but that is indistinguishable from the `auto-executed-tool-call` scenario. The thing that DEFINES auto-classified-permission — a classifier that diverts a risky call into `working → waiting` within the same session — does not exist in aider, so there is no behavior to record. Hence `n/a` rather than `no`: it is not a transport gap to fix, it is that the agent has nothing classifier-shaped to observe.

## What would change this

If aider ever shipped a content-aware command-safety classifier (selectively prompting only for risky shell commands while auto-running safe ones), this would become `agent_supports: yes` and `irrlicht_observes` would need a parser change: the markdown history would have to surface a confirmation prompt the parser could map to a held-open tool_use, since today aider's interactive idle confirmation prompt never lands in `.aider.chat.history.md` at all.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: even the blanket --yes-always confirmation prompt is not written to .aider.chat.history.md, so irrlicht has no transcript signal for any permission decision, classifier-based or not.

The safe-path topology (ready→working→ready) IS observable, but it is identical to auto-executed-tool-call; the classifier's risky-path divergence is what is unobservable, and it does not exist in aider.

sources.note: toolCall() (lines 231-242) emits tool open+close in one tool_result "without a permission window"; no parser path emits a waiting/permission event
Source is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; adapter assumes aider runs under --yes-always
confirm_ask (line 807): only decision is self.yes True/False/interactive (lines 866-868) — uniform, no content classifier
--yes-always (line 760) "Always say yes to every confirmation" — blanket; only auto-* flags are --yes-always and --auto-accept-architect, neither content-aware
Aider 0.86.2 options reference: --yes-always is the sole blanket-approve flag; no safe-vs-risky classifier documented
